name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 23.02.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 23.02.2026.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: Summer sowing data collection has been started from 9 th  February, 2026 and the sowing is in preliminary stage. As on February 20, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 21.30 lakh ha as compared to 20.38 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 17.78 lakh ha has been sown under rice this year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: Summer sowing data collection has been started from 9 th  February, 2026 and the sowing is in preliminary stage. As on February 20, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 21.30 lakh ha as compared to 20.38 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 17.78 lakh ha has been sown under rice this year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 20.02.2026, 99% of area has been harvested for Kharif crops all over India.
texts.text:  As on 20.02.2026, 99% of area has been harvested for Kharif crops all over India.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal over most parts of the country except East India, central and north peninsula where they are likely to be below normal.  Maximum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  most  parts  of  the country except central & adjoining peninsular India.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal over most parts of the country except East India, central and north peninsula where they are likely to be below normal.  Maximum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  most  parts  of  the country except central & adjoining peninsular India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- February 22 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  58%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  92%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  48%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  82%;  and South Peninsula: (-) 26%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- February 22 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  58%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  92%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  48%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  82%;  and South Peninsula: (-) 26%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Isolated light/moderate rainfall with thunderstorm, lightning & gusty winds likely over Odisha on 23 rd  & 24 th ; with thunderstorm & lightning likely over East Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha,  Chhattisgarh  on  23 rd ;  Assam  &  Meghalaya,  Gangetic  West  Bengal  and Telangana on 23 rd  & 24 th  February.
texts.text: Isolated light/moderate rainfall with thunderstorm, lightning & gusty winds likely over Odisha on 23 rd  & 24 th ; with thunderstorm & lightning likely over East Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha,  Chhattisgarh  on  23 rd ;  Assam  &  Meghalaya,  Gangetic  West  Bengal  and Telangana on 23 rd  & 24 th  February.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d) Isolated rainfall/snowfall likely over Himachal Pradesh on 23rd and Uttarakhand on 23 rd & 24 th  February.
texts.text: Isolated rainfall/snowfall likely over Himachal Pradesh on 23rd and Uttarakhand on 23 rd & 24 th  February.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e) Overall, rainfall is likely to be below normal over most parts of the country except Tamil Nadu, Kerala and some parts of Karnataka, where above normal rainfall activities very likely during the week.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall is likely to be below normal over most parts of the country except Tamil Nadu, Kerala and some parts of Karnataka, where above normal rainfall activities very likely during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual rise in maximum temperature by 3-4°C likely over Northwest India during next 7days.  No  significant  change  in  maximum  temperatures  likely  over  rest  parts  of  the country.
texts.text:  Gradual rise in maximum temperature by 3-4°C likely over Northwest India during next 7days.  No  significant  change  in  maximum  temperatures  likely  over  rest  parts  of  the country.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  19  February  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  113.160  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  62%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is nearly 111.55 percent of last year's storage and  125.37 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  19  February  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  113.160  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  62%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is nearly 111.55 percent of last year's storage and  125.37 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon 2025, around 90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon 2025, around 90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.
texts.text:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.
texts.text:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
